description:
You are a QA engineer preparing a clean “remote-test” snapshot of the current source tree so that other engineers can reproduce today’s test results.  
    A skeleton of the project already exists in /home/user/source_env.  
    Your job is to synchronise that tree to a brand-new directory called /home/user/qa_remote_env and then write two short text files that will let the CI system verify what happened.

    1. Create the empty directory /home/user/qa_remote_env.  
       (It must not contain any files when rsync starts.)

    2. Use rsync (or another single-command file-synchronisation tool) to copy
       everything that is inside /home/user/source_env **into** /home/user/qa_remote_env.  
       • Use archive/verbose options so that regular file attributes are preserved.  
       • Use the “--delete” style option so that if a file is later removed from the
         source_env it would also disappear from qa_remote_env on the next sync.  
       • You are free to choose the exact flags as long as those three properties
         (archive behaviour, verbosity, deletion) are satisfied.

    3. Capture the complete console output that rsync prints and write it, verbatim,
       to a new file called /home/user/sync_logs/rsync_1.log with the following format:

           RSYNC LOG START
           … (all lines exactly as rsync printed them) …
           RSYNC LOG END

       The first line **must** be the literal text “RSYNC LOG START”  
       The last  line **must** be the literal text “RSYNC LOG END”  

    4. Verify that the two directories are byte-for-byte identical after the copy.
       You may use “diff -r”, “rsync --dry-run”, “find | md5sum”, or any other tool.
       Write a single-line verification report to /home/user/sync_logs/verification_report.txt.

       • If the directories are identical, the file must contain exactly:
             Directories are in sync.
       • If they are not, write:
             Directories differ.

    The automated test will check:

       a) /home/user/qa_remote_env exists and contains the *same* files & sub-directories
          as /home/user/source_env.  
       b) /home/user/sync_logs/rsync_1.log exists and:
            – first line  == “RSYNC LOG START”  
            – last line   == “RSYNC LOG END”  
            – has at least one rsync payload line in between.  
       c) /home/user/sync_logs/verification_report.txt exists and contains exactly the
          expected single sentence (case-sensitive, no extra spaces or newline chars
          before or after the line).

    You do NOT need to compress archives, edit any source files, or change permissions.
    Accomplish the whole task with the minimum number of reasonable shell commands
    (it will typically take three to four commands).
truth:
<!--
        The container image used for grading already contains this directory tree:

        /home/user/source_env
        ├── app.py               (text file, 3 lines)
        ├── README.md            (text file, 1 line)
        ├── requirements.txt     (text file, 2 lines)
        └── data
            └── sample.json      (text file, 1 line)

        Exact file contents:

        /home/user/source_env/app.py
            print("Hello QA")

        /home/user/source_env/README.md
            Example project for rsync test

        /home/user/source_env/requirements.txt
            flask==2.0.2
            pytest==7.4.0

        /home/user/source_env/data/sample.json
            { "value": 42 }

        The /home/user/sync_logs directory does NOT exist initially; the agent must create it.

        After correct execution:

        1. /home/user/qa_remote_env will exist and have the same four files
           with identical relative paths and byte-for-byte identical content.

        2. /home/user/sync_logs/rsync_1.log will exist and:
           • first line  -> RSYNC LOG START\n
           • last  line  -> RSYNC LOG END\n
           • at least one non-empty line between them

        3. /home/user/sync_logs/verification_report.txt will exist and contain exactly:
              Directories are in sync.\n
    -->
